product_overview: CloudVault is a lightweight cloud file manager that stores file blobs in Telegram (via TDLib) and file metadata/auth in Supabase. It supports guest and registered users with upload, streaming download (range support), and metadata CRUD + sharing.
core_goals: Provide fast, low-friction file uploads and streaming downloads using Telegram as storage backend
Support guest sessions and registered users with secure metadata stored in Supabase (RLS)
Enable reliable large-file uploads via chunked uploads and server-side assembly
Expose metadata CRUD and shareable links while enforcing auth and RLS
Keep API surface resilient to TDLib/Telegram availability with clear error paths and fallbacks
Deliver realtime updates (Supabase) and responsive UI performance targets

features.name: File Upload API
features.description: Accepts file uploads (single and chunked), forwards file data to TDLib/Telegram, and persists metadata in Supabase.
user_flows: POST /api/upload with multipart/form-data (file, optional guest_session_id or Authorization: Bearer <JWT>) -> Receive 200 with file metadata including id, telegram_file_id and size_bytes
POST /api/upload with multipart/form-data and same filename -> Receive 409 Conflict (name-based deduplication detected)
POST /chunked-upload/init with Authorization: Bearer <JWT> and file metadata -> Receive 201 with upload_session_id and expected_chunk_size
POST /chunked-upload/{upload_session_id} with chunk bytes and Authorization: Bearer <JWT> -> Receive 200 chunk-ack
POST /chunked-upload/{upload_session_id} with final chunk and Authorization: Bearer <JWT> -> Receive 200 chunk-ack
POST /chunked-upload/complete with upload_session_id and Authorization: Bearer <JWT> -> Receive 200 with assembled file metadata including telegram_file_id
POST /chunked-upload/init without Authorization header -> Receive 401 Unauthorized
POST /api/upload with multipart/form-data when TDLib service is unavailable -> Receive 502 Bad Gateway (TDLib unavailable) and upload failure status

features.name: File Download and Streaming API
features.description: Serves files to clients via tokenized public paths or authenticated proxies. Supports HTTP Range requests and cache-aware backend streaming (via TDLib).
user_flows: GET /file/[...params] with valid signed tokenized path -> Receive 200 with streamed file and Accept-Ranges header
GET /file/[...params] with valid signed tokenized path and Range: bytes=0-1023 -> Receive 206 Partial Content with requested byte range
GET /api/download/[...params] with Authorization: Bearer <JWT> and Range header -> Receive 206 Partial Content streaming bytes (auth-scoped proxy)
GET /download/:id with Authorization: Bearer <JWT> -> Backend checks cache -> If cached: Receive 200 with streamed file from cache

GET /download/:id with Authorization: Bearer <JWT> -> Backend checks cache -> If not cached: Backend calls TDLib to get temporary download URL -> TDLib returns temp URL -> Receive 200 with streamed file after backend fetch
GET /file/[...params] with expired or tampered token -> Receive 403 Forbidden (invalid/expired signed token)
GET /download/:id with Authorization: Bearer <JWT> when file metadata missing in Supabase -> Receive 404 Not Found
GET /download/:id with Authorization: Bearer <JWT> when TDLib returns 404 or error during fetch -> Receive 502 Bad Gateway (TDLib fetch failed)

features.name: Metadata CRUD API
features.description: List, update and manage file and folder metadata and create/resolve share tokens stored in Supabase (enforced by RLS).
user_flows: GET /api/files with Authorization: Bearer <JWT> and optional query ?folder_id=<id> -> Receive 200 with array of files filtered by folder and user ownership
PATCH /api/files with Authorization: Bearer <JWT> and body { id:<file_id>, name: "new-name" } -> Receive 200 with updated file metadata (name changed)
GET /api/folders with Authorization: Bearer <JWT> -> Receive 200 with folders list for current user or guest session
POST /api/share with Authorization: Bearer <JWT> and body { file_id:<id>, expires_at:<ts|null> } -> Receive 201 with share token
GET /api/share/[token] (public) -> Receive 200 with resolved file metadata and optional public download path
GET /api/files without Authorization header -> Receive 401 Unauthorized
PATCH /api/files with Authorization: Bearer <JWT> for a file not owned by the user -> Receive 403 Forbidden (RLS prevents modification)
POST /api/share with Authorization: Bearer <JWT> for a file that user is not permitted to share -> Receive 403 Forbidden
GET /api/share/[token] for an invalid or expired token -> Receive 404 Not Found

features.name: File Upload API
features.description: Uploads files to Telegram storage through the TDLib service and stores metadata in Supabase.

endpoints.description: Upload a file with multipart form data.
Initialize chunked upload session for large files.
Complete chunked upload and send assembled file to Telegram.

features.name: File Download and Streaming API
features.description: Streams files by id with range support and cache-aware fallback paths.

endpoints.description: Public file stream route using signed tokenized path.
Backend stream endpoint with cache and range handling.

features.name: Metadata CRUD API
features.description: Manages file and folder metadata and sharing records in Supabase.

endpoints.description: List files with filters for folder, starred and trashed state.
Update file metadata such as rename, move, trash and starred flags.
List folders for current user or guest session.
Create a public share token for a file.
Resolve shared token metadata and optional download path.

known_limitations.issue: Upload concurrency limiters are duplicated across single and chunked upload handlers.
known_limitations.impact: Parallel uploads can be throttled inconsistently.

known_limitations.issue: Upload deduplication is currently name-based instead of content-hash based.
known_limitations.impact: Duplicate content with different names is stored repeatedly.

known_limitations.issue: Chunked upload sessions are in-memory and do not survive restarts.
known_limitations.impact: In-progress large uploads fail after backend restart.
